blocks.content: First practice. The difference between tryouts and practice: tryouts you're auditioning. Practice you're already there — now you have to prove you belong.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: Welcome to the team. I don't say that again. From here the baseline is you belong here. Now we find out how good.

He ran us for forty minutes before we touched a ball. By the time routes started, everyone on the field had been tested in a different way than tryouts. Tryouts measure what you have. Practice measures what you do with it when you're already tired.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Aye stay on my route timing. When I break, you break. Don't improvise yet — just pattern first.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You're going to tell me what to do in practice?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I'm going to keep you from getting embarrassed on the first day. There's a difference.
There was. I ran his routes. By the third rep I started feeling the timing in my legs instead of my head — the way Jonathan said it would happen.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: Jenkins. You're learning the route tree or running it?

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: Then run it like you believe that.
I ran the next one different. I stopped thinking about whether I was doing it right and just ran.
Coach Briggs watched. Wrote something down. Said nothing.
From Coach Briggs that was apparently everything.
